channel.title: REST on Work In Progress
channel.description: Recent content in REST on Work In Progress

item.title: Doing more with less
item.description:
Episode 3: Like what you’re done with that rabbit hole

Previously I wrote about the architectural pattern I was intending use for this adventure and I did make a start; honest.

I took initial steps to move communications into two functions, one for tfl and the other for Neo4j but…

Along came a squirrel.

Not a real one like that in the picture and really not this one 1 but a virtual one wearing a large badge of distraction that read ‘I wonder if I could get all the information needed from tfl API in fewer calls?’

item.title: It is all about the REST
item.description:
It is all about the REST

Neo4j DB as a Service ( DBaaS ) , Aura, now has a RESTful like API for provisioning. The full details of the API in long form are in the documentation; in short it’s a set of endpoints that provide for CRUD operations. You can try the Aura API from the documentation. Here we’re going to dig in and write code to do the needful.
